title: The Artificial Neuron
caption: A McCulloch–Pitts threshold neuron: inputs summed against a fixed threshold θ.

overview.text: In 1943, neurophysiologist Warren McCulloch and logician Walter Pitts proposed that the brain's neurons could be modelled as simple logical switches. Each idealized neuron fired only when its inputs crossed a threshold — and networks of them, they showed, could in principle compute anything a logical statement could express.

deep.text: A McCulloch–Pitts unit takes binary inputs, sums them, and outputs 1 only when the sum meets a fixed threshold θ. With excitatory and inhibitory connections you can wire AND, OR and NOT gates — and therefore any Boolean function. What is missing is learning: the weights and threshold are set by hand, not discovered from data.
deep.math: y = 1  if  Σᵢ xᵢ ≥ θ,  else 0

sources.title: A Logical Calculus of the Ideas Immanent in Nervous Activity
sources.author: W. McCulloch & W. Pitts
